content: In the context of construction materials, which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?

options.content: If the characteristic strength is defined as that value below which not more than $50 \%$ results are expected to fall, the target mean strength in mix design will be taken same as the characteristic strength irrespective of the degree of quality control expected at the site.
Ten percent fines value is a non-dimensional quantity.

The stress-strain curve of concrete for 1-day duration of loading is associated with a smaller secant modulus of elasticity compared to the stress-strain curve of the same concrete for 10-minutes duration of loading.
The increase of carbon in steel usually leads to an increase in its $0.2 \%$ proof stress.

explanation:
The correct expression as per IS $456: 2000$ to find Target mean strength is :

$$ \begin{aligned} & \quad f_{c m}=f_{c k}+(k \times \sigma) \\ & f_{c m}=\text { Target mean strength } \\ & f_{c k}=\text { Characteristics strength } \\ & \sigma=\text { Standard deviation } \end{aligned} $$

For not more than $50 \%$ of results are likely to fall $k=0$.

Since characteristic strength has been defined as the value below which not more than $50 \%$ of results are likely to fall.

Hence, Target mean strength

$$ \begin{aligned} & =f_t=f_{c k}+(0 \times \sigma)=\text { characteristics strength } \left(f_{c k}\right)=25 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{mm}^2 . \end{aligned} $$

Hence, the correct option is (A), (C) and (D).

The initial cost of an equipment is Rs. 1,00,000. Its salvage value at the end of accounting life of 5 years is Rs. 10,000. The difference in depreciation (in Rs.) computed using ‘double-declining balance method’ and ‘straight line method’ of depreciation in Year-2 is ___________ (in positive integer).
explanation:
To solve this problem, we need to calculate the depreciation for Year-2 using both the Double-Declining Balance (DDB) method and the Straight-Line (SL) method, and then find the difference between the two.

Firstly, let's calculate the annual depreciation using the Straight-Line method.

The formula for straight-line depreciation is:

$$ \text{Depreciation}_{SL} = \frac{\text{Initial Cost} - \text{Salvage Value}}{\text{Useful Life}} $$

Given:

  • Initial Cost = Rs. 1,00,000
  • Salvage Value = Rs. 10,000
  • Useful Life = 5 years

Thus,

$$ \text{Depreciation}_{SL} = \frac{1,00,000 - 10,000}{5} = \frac{90,000}{5} = 18,000 \, \text{Rs./year} $$

Now, let's calculate the depreciation using the Double-Declining Balance method.

The formula for double-declining balance depreciation is:

$$ \text{Depreciation}_{DDB} = 2 \times \text{Straight-Line Depreciation Rate} \times \text{Book Value at Beginning of Year} $$

The Straight-Line Depreciation Rate is:

$$ \text{Straight-Line Depreciation Rate} = \frac{1}{\text{Useful Life}} = \frac{1}{5} = 0.2 $$

Therefore, the Double-Declining Balance Depreciation Rate is:

$$ \text{DDB Rate} = 2 \times 0.2 = 0.4 = 40\% $$

Let's now compute the depreciation for Year-1 and Year-2 using the DDB method.

Year-1:

$$ \text{Depreciation}_{\text{DDB-1}} = 0.4 \times 1,00,000 = 40,000 \, \text{Rs.} $$

Year-2:

$$ \text{Book Value}_{\text{beginning of Year-2}} = 1,00,000 - 40,000 = 60,000 \, \text{Rs.} $$

Therefore,

$$ \text{Depreciation}_{\text{DDB-2}} = 0.4 \times 60,000 = 24,000 \, \text{Rs.} $$

Now, we can find the difference in depreciation for Year-2 between the two methods:

$$ \text{Difference} = \text{Depreciation}_{\text{DDB-2}} - \text{Depreciation}_{\text{SL}} = 24,000 - 18,000 = 6,000 \, \text{Rs.} $$

Therefore, the difference in depreciation computed using the Double-Declining Balance method and the Straight-Line method in Year-2 is 6,000 Rs.

content:
The composition of an air-entrained concrete is given below:

Water : 184 kg /m3

Ordinary Portland Cement(OPC) : 368 kg /m3

Sand : 606 kg /m3

Coarse aggregate : 1155 kg /m3

Assume the specific gravity of OPC, sand and coarse aggregate to be 3.14, 2.67 and 2.74, respectively. The air content is_________ liters/m3

According to the concept of Limit State Design as per IS 456: 2000, the probability of failure of a
structure is __________.

Consider a reinforcing bar embedded in concrete. In a marine environment this bar undergoes
uniform corrosion, which leads to deposition of corrosion products on its surface and an
increase in apparent volume of the bar. This subjects the surrounding concrete to expansive
pressure. As a result, corrosion cracks appear at surface of concrete. Which of the following
statement is true?

options.content:
Corrosion causes circumferential tensile stress in concrete and the cracks will be parallel
to corroded reinforcing bar.

Corrosion causes radial tensile stresses in concrete and cracks will be parallel to corroded
reinforcing bar.

Corrosion causes circumferential tensile stresses in concrete and cracks will be
perpendicular to the direction corroded reinforcing bar.

Corrosion causes radial tensile stresses in concrete and cracks will be perpendicular to
direction of the corroded reinforcing bar.
